allies.rationale_text: Social-proof helps commitment-and-consistency-bias spread because public alignment with a group makes later reversal feel more costly.
allies.source_quote: Loyalty and the craving to belong to a community

allies.rationale_text: Sunk-cost-fallacy reinforces commitment-and-consistency-bias by making prior investment feel like a reason to keep going.
allies.source_quote: What would I do if I had no sunk costs invested in this outcome?

antagonists.rationale_text: Intellectual-humility interrupts commitment-and-consistency-bias by making truth more valuable than preserving a prior public stance.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Commitment-and-consistency-bias conflicts with optionality when public consistency pressure forecloses the ability to pivot when new information favors a different path.
structured_tensions.source_quote: commitment-and-consistency-bias conflicts with optionality (optionality) when public consistency pressure forecloses the ability to pivot when new information favors a different path.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Commitment-and-consistency-bias conflicts with scientific-method-evidence-testing when the need to honor prior commitments overrides willingness to update beliefs based on new experimental evidence.
structured_tensions.source_quote: commitment-and-consistency-bias conflicts with scientific-method-evidence-testing (scientific method evidence testing) when the need to honor prior commitments overrides willingness to update beliefs based on new experimental evidence.

curation_notes.summary: The relation file stays on the clearest first-order graph: social and sunk-cost reinforcement against humility-driven updating.
donor_drops: Did not manufacture explicit tensions because the raw file is stronger on bias mechanics than on named tension blocks.
open_questions: The social-proof ally is interpretive but remains well grounded in the raw source's community and loyalty material.
